error.signature: error CS0246: The type or namespace name could not be found (are you missing an assembly reference?)
error.regex: (CS0246|type or namespace.*could not be found|missing.*assembly reference|assembly.*not found|asmdef.*missing)

verdict.summary: C# compiler cannot find a type. Missing package, wrong assembly definition, or namespace not imported.

dead_ends.action: Copy the DLL file directly into the Assets folder
dead_ends.why_fails: Unity has a package manager. Manual DLLs create version conflicts and are not tracked.

dead_ends.action: Remove all .asmdef files to fix assembly resolution
dead_ends.why_fails: Assembly definitions control compilation boundaries. Removing them can cause circular dependency errors.

workarounds.action: Add the missing package via Unity Package Manager
workarounds.how: Window > Package Manager > search for the package; or edit Packages/manifest.json

workarounds.action: Add using directive for the correct namespace
workarounds.how: using UnityEngine.UI;  // for UI components; using TMPro;  // for TextMeshPro
Edit your .asmdef to include the dependency assembly in Assembly Definition References
